info.name: 🏦 ACH Payment AI Triage System — Complete Test Suite
info.description:
Complete test collection for all API endpoints of the ACH Payment & Positive Pay AI Triage System v3.0.

## How to use:
1. Set the `baseUrl` variable (default: http://localhost:3001)
2. Run **01 - Auth / Login** first to auto-set the `authToken` variable
3. Run folders in order for a full end-to-end test flow

## Variables:
- `baseUrl`  — API base (http://localhost:3001)
- `authToken` — auto-filled by login request
- `txnId`    — auto-filled by create transaction request
- `jobId`    — auto-filled by bulk upload request
- `mirToken` — set manually from MIR portal response

request.description: Verify server is alive. Check Gemini connection status.
Login as admin. Auto-saves JWT token to `authToken` variable.
Should return 401 Invalid credentials.
Missing password — should return 400.
Returns logged-in user profile from JWT.
Admin creates a new user. Roles: reviewer, analyst, admin, supervisor.

Admin: list all users in the system.
Update a user's role or active status. Replace USR-REPLACE_ME with actual user_id.
Force reset password for a user. New password will be emailed (or logged to console).
Any authenticated user can change their own password.
Permanently delete a user. Cannot delete yourself.
Paginated list of all transactions.
Filter: under_review, auto_approved, approved, declined, more_info_required, ai_workflow

item.name: 10 — Pattern Promotion, Demotion & AI Autonomous Workflow
item.description:
Tests the entire AI Learning Pipeline lifecycle:

PROMOTION PATH:
  ≥5 decisions on the same pattern type + confidence ≥85% → _promotePattern() fires
  → workflow_playbook is built → future transactions run autonomous AI workflow

DEMOTION PATH:
  After promotion, if confidence drops < 70% → _demotePattern() fires
  → promoted_to_level1 = false, workflow_playbook = null

AUTONOMOUS WORKFLOW PATH (ai_workflow status):
  New txn matches promoted pattern → runAutonomousWorkflow() starts
  → AI sends MIR portal → originator responds → evaluateOriginatorResponse() → AI approve/decline/escalate

HOW TO RUN:
  These calls simulate real human decisions on the same transaction 'type'.
  The system groups by: sec_code + transaction_type + amount_bucket + risk_flags + account_type.
  Run steps in order. After 5 'approve' decisions, the pattern will auto-promote.

request.description: Record baseline: totalPatterns, promotedPatterns, autonomousPatterns before the test.
See which patterns exist and their current confidence scores before testing.

item.name: GET  Audit Log — Filter Pattern Events (before)

request.description: Look for any existing pattern_promoted or pattern_demoted events in the audit log.

item.name: ── STEP 2: Seed the Pattern (5 identical-type decisions) ──
item.description:
Create 5 transactions of the same 'pattern type': same sec_code=CCD, transaction_type=debit, amount bucket=small (500–5000), account_type=checking, no flags.
Approve each one. After 5 approvals, confidence_score ≥ 0.85 → PROMOTION fires automatically.

item.name: POST Create Pattern Txn #1 (CCD debit small)

request.description: Pattern seed #1 — CCD debit $2,500 (small bucket). Same pattern fingerprint as #2–#5.

  "decision": "approve",

  "business_purpose": "regular_vendor_payment",
  "authorization_reviewed": true,

}

item.name: POST Approve Pattern Txn #5 → TRIGGERS PROMOTION

body.raw:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "decision_reason": "Fifth consecutive approval of same pattern — meets promotion criteria.",
  "reviewer_confidence": "HIGH",
  "identity_verified": true,
  "identity_verification_method": "signed_agreement",
  "counterparty_type": "BUSINESS",
  "business_purpose": "regular_vendor_payment",
  "authorization_reviewed": true,
  "additional_notes": "Pattern seed decision 5 of 5 — promotion expected after this",
  "time_to_decide_seconds": 102
}

request.description:
🚀 PROMOTION TRIGGER: After this 5th HIGH-confidence approve, _promotePattern() fires automatically.
Check the audit log immediately after — look for event_type: 'pattern_promoted'.

✅ EXPECTED: event_type='pattern_promoted' in the top results.
Event summary: '🚀 Pattern XXXX promoted — autonomous workflow enabled (85%+ confidence, 5 decisions)'
✅ EXPECTED: promotedPatterns count increased by 1, autonomousPatterns count increased by 1.
✅ EXPECTED: The CCD debit small pattern now has: promoted_to_level1=true, workflow_playbook not null, promotion_date set.

item.name: ── STEP 4: AI Autonomous Workflow (Post-Promotion) ──
item.description: Now create a NEW transaction of the same type. Because the pattern is promoted, this transaction goes into 'ai_workflow' status instead of 'under_review'. The AI will autonomously send MIR requests.

item.name: POST Create Txn Matching Promoted Pattern → ai_workflow

exec: console.log('⚡ Auto-approved (no MIR playbook needed for this risk score)');

request.description:
Same pattern fingerprint → matches promoted pattern.
✅ EXPECTED response message: '🤖 AI autonomous workflow started — originator will be contacted'
✅ EXPECTED status: 'ai_workflow'

✅ EXPECTED: status='ai_workflow', ai_workflow_pattern= pattern hash, ai_human_override=false.
Audit logs should show: ai_workflow_queued → ai_workflow_started → ai_info_requested

item.name: GET  AI Workflow MIR Info Requests

request.description: ✅ EXPECTED: At least one info_request with actor_type='AI_AUTOMATION'. Portal token is masked (••••••••).

item.name: POST Human Override — Stop AI, Take Control

request.description:
Human reviewer takes over from AI workflow.
✅ EXPECTED: ai_human_override=true, status flipped to 'under_review'.
Audit log: event_type='human_override'

item.name: ── STEP 5: Trigger Demotion ──
item.description:
To demote a promoted pattern, we need confidence to fall below 70%.
This means consecutive DECLINE decisions on the same pattern type, using MEDIUM/LOW reviewer confidence.
After enough decline decisions, confidence_score < 0.70 → _demotePattern() fires.

item.name: POST Create Pattern Txn for Demotion #1

request.description: Same pattern as promoted — will enter ai_workflow. We'll override and then decline.

item.name: POST Override AI on Demotion Txn #1

request.description: Take manual control so we can issue a human decline decision.

item.name: POST DECLINE Demotion Txn #1 — LOW confidence

body.raw:
{
  "decision": "decline",
  "decision_reason": "Suspicious activity — originator could not verify authorization.",
  "reviewer_confidence": "LOW",
  "recommended_return_code": "R05",
  "fraud_indicators": ["unauthorized_debit", "no_authorization_on_file"],
  "counterparty_type": "BUSINESS",
  "business_purpose": "unknown",
  "additional_notes": "Demotion seed decision — LOW confidence decline",
  "time_to_decide_seconds": 200
}

request.description: Decline with LOW reviewer_confidence (weight=0.4). This reduces approve_weight relative to decline_weight, lowering confidence_score.

item.name: POST Create Pattern Txn for Demotion #2

request.description: Demotion seed #2.

item.name: POST Override AI on Demotion Txn #2

request.description: Human takes over from AI.

item.name: POST DECLINE Demotion Txn #2 — LOW confidence

body.raw:
{
  "decision": "decline",
  "decision_reason": "Repeated suspicious pattern — second consecutive decline.",
  "reviewer_confidence": "LOW",
  "recommended_return_code": "R10",
  "fraud_indicators": ["unauthorized_debit", "pattern_mismatch"],
  "counterparty_type": "BUSINESS",
  "business_purpose": "unknown",
  "additional_notes": "Demotion seed decision 2",
  "time_to_decide_seconds": 185
}

request.description:
Second LOW-confidence decline. Confidence formula: approveW / (approveW + declineW).
With 5 HIGH approves (weight=5.0) and 2 LOW declines (weight=0.8), confidence = 5.0/5.8 ≈ 86% → still above 70%.
Keep adding declines until confidence < 70% to trigger demotion.

Check current confidence_score. If < 0.70, 'pattern_demoted' event should be in audit log.
Formula: approve_weight / (approve_weight + decline_weight). HIGH=1.0, MEDIUM=0.7, LOW=0.4

item.name: GET  Audit Log — Check for 'pattern_demoted' Event

request.description:
✅ EXPECTED (if confidence < 0.70): event_type='pattern_demoted'
Event summary: '⬇️ Pattern XXXX DEMOTED — autonomous workflow disabled (confidence: XX%)'

If not yet demoted: add more LOW-confidence decline decisions until confidence drops below 70%.

item.name: POST Create Txn After Demotion — Should NOT trigger ai_workflow

exec: console.log('✅ Demotion confirmed — pattern no longer triggers autonomous workflow');
console.log('❌ Pattern still promoted — need more decline decisions');
console.log('⚡ Auto-approved due to low risk score');

request.description:
After demotion, the same pattern type should:
✅ EXPECTED: status='under_review' (human review required again)
❌ NOT EXPECTED: status='ai_workflow' (autonomous workflow disabled after demotion)

Final state check:
- promotedPatterns: should be same as before (demotion removed 1)
- autonomousPatterns: should have decreased
- The demoted pattern: promoted_to_level1=false, demotion_count incremented, workflow_playbook=null

body.raw:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "decision_reason": "Transaction appears legitimate with moderate certainty.",
  "reviewer_confidence": "MEDIUM",
  "identity_verified": false,
  "counterparty_type": "BUSINESS",
  "business_purpose": "vendor_payment",
  "additional_notes": "MEDIUM confidence — contributes 0.7 weight to pattern confidence",
  "time_to_decide_seconds": 300
}

request.description:
MEDIUM confidence (weight=0.7) approve — contributes less to pattern confidence than HIGH (1.0).
Replace {{txnId}} with an under_review transaction.

body.raw:
{
  "decision": "decline",
  "decision_reason": "Uncertain — partial information only.",
  "reviewer_confidence": "LOW",
  "recommended_return_code": "R03",
  "fraud_indicators": ["insufficient_documentation"],
  "counterparty_type": "UNKNOWN",
  "business_purpose": "unknown",
  "additional_notes": "LOW confidence — contributes 0.4 weight, minimal demotion pressure",
  "time_to_decide_seconds": 450
}

request.description: LOW confidence (weight=0.4) decline — minimal impact on pattern demotion. Takes many more to demote.

item.name: GET  Resubmission Guard — Same Txn Second Decision (expect 409)

request.description:
Try to approve an already-approved transaction.
✅ EXPECTED: 409 Conflict — 'Already in status: approved'
Learning pipeline guard: resubmission deduplication ensures only the FIRST terminal decision counts.

item.name: GET  Pattern Stats After Full Test

request.description:
Final pattern state. Verify:
- total_decisions count
- approve_count / decline_count
- confidence_score
- promoted_to_level1 flag
- demotion_count
- workflow_playbook (null if demoted)

item.name: 11 — MIR + Pattern Learning Full E2E Flow
item.description:
End-to-end scenario: Create high-risk transaction → request info → originator responds → human decides → pattern learns from MIR lifecycle.
This tests that MIR rounds (request+response steps) are captured in the lifecycle and included in learned_qa_pairs for the workflow_playbook.

item.name: POST Create High-Risk Txn for MIR+Learning E2E

request.description: $45,000 CCD debit (large bucket) — likely Level 2 or 3 → under_review. We'll do a full MIR round.
Round 1 MIR — AUTHORIZATION_PROOF. This records a lifecycle step and builds learned_qa_pairs.

item.name: GET  Portal Link from Info Requests

request.description:
Get the MIR round details. The portal_token is masked, but the portal_url is logged on the server.
Copy the token from server console logs to set {{mirToken}} variable.

item.name: POST Portal Respond — Originator Submits Proof

request.description:
Originator responds with detailed authorization proof.
This records a lifecycle response step. Status flips to under_review.
Set {{mirToken}} from server console output or MIR info-requests response.

item.name: POST Approve with Full Review Data — Finalizes Lifecycle

body.raw:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "decision_reason": "Authorization proof received and verified — signed agreement on file, CFO signature confirmed.",
  "reviewer_confidence": "HIGH",
  "identity_verified": true,
  "identity_verification_method": "signed_agreement",
  "counterparty_type": "BUSINESS",
  "account_ownership_confirmed": true,
  "business_purpose": "contract_payment",
  "authorization_reviewed": true,
  "authorization_type_confirmed": "written",
  "customer_contacted": true,
  "customer_contact_outcome": "confirmed_authorization",
  "additional_notes": "E2E test — MIR lifecycle with 1 round and final HIGH-confidence approval",
  "time_to_decide_seconds": 3600
}

request.description:
Final decision with full reviewer data.
✅ This triggers: finaliseLifecycle() → _updatePatternFromLifecycle() → learned_qa_pairs updated with request+response.
The new Q&A pair (AUTHORIZATION_PROOF request + originator response) is added to the pattern's training data.

item.name: GET  Transaction Detail — Full Audit Trail

request.description:
Complete transaction history including audit_logs and review_decisions.
✅ EXPECTED audit log events in order:
1. transaction_created
2. ai_processed
3. info_requested (Round 1)
4. originator_response_submitted
5. transaction_resubmitted
6. human_approved

item.name: GET  Pattern — Verify MIR Q&A Pair Recorded

request.description:
After completing the E2E flow, find the pattern for CCD debit large bucket.
✅ EXPECTED: learned_qa_pairs contains the new entry with:
  - category: 'AUTHORIZATION_PROOF'
  - message_template: (the request message)
  - response_example: (the originator response)
  - final_outcome: 'approve'
✅ mir_count incremented, avg_rounds_to_resolve updated.

item.name: 12 — 🎬 AI AUTONOMOUS MIR DEMO (Watch AI Work Live)
item.description:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎬  LIVE AI DEMO — More Info Required (Fully Autonomous)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This folder is a complete, self-contained DEMO that shows:

  1️⃣  Pattern already learned from 5 prior human approvals (seeded here)
  2️⃣  New transaction arrives → AI recognises the pattern
  3️⃣  AI AUTOMATICALLY sends a More Info Request to the originator
  4️⃣  Originator opens the portal link and submits their response
  5️⃣  AI evaluates the response using Gemini LLM + learned examples
  6️⃣  AI makes final decision: APPROVE / DECLINE / ask again
  7️⃣  Full audit trail proves every AI action

NO HUMAN REVIEWER IS NEEDED IN STEPS 3–6. The AI does it all.

RUN ORDER: Execute requests top-to-bottom. Watch the server console
for 🤖 AI_AUTOMATION log lines in real time.

NOTE: Steps A1-A5 seed the pattern (run once). Steps B onwards are
the live demo you repeat to see the AI in action.

item.name: ━━ PHASE A: Teach AI the MIR Pattern (5 Full Cycles — Run Once) ━━
item.description:
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🎓 TEACHING THE AI — MIR-FIRST PATTERN
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

For each of the 5 seed transactions, do the COMPLETE MIR lifecycle:

  1. CREATE transaction
  2. HUMAN requests info (AUTHORIZATION_PROOF)
  3. ORIGINATOR responds with strong authorization details via portal
  4. HUMAN approves after seeing the good response

Why this matters:
  ❌ OLD (wrong): direct approve → AI learns 'just approve'
  ✅ NEW (correct): request MIR → get good response → approve
     → AI learns: 'for this pattern, always ask for AUTHORIZATION_PROOF
       and approve when a strong signed-agreement response is given'

After 5 cycles, the workflow_playbook will contain:
  round 1: { category: AUTHORIZATION_PROOF, expected_decision: approve }

When the AI runs autonomously later, it will EXACTLY replicate
this human behaviour — ask for proof, evaluate, approve/decline.

⏱ EACH SEED TAKES ~4 API CALLS. Total: ~20 calls for full training.

📌 PORTAL TOKEN: After each 'Request Info' call, check your server
console for the portal link. Copy the token into {{mirTokenSeedN}}.

This playbook is EXACTLY what the AI will replay autonomously in Phase B.

item.name: ━━ PHASE B: 🎬 THE LIVE DEMO (Repeat this anytime) ━━
item.description:
Phase A only needs to run once.
From here, this is the REPEATABLE DEMO you show to anyone.

What will happen:
  • You submit a new transaction of the SAME type
  • AI detects the promoted pattern INSTANTLY
  • AI changes status to 'ai_workflow'
  • AI generates and sends a More Info Request automatically
  • You (playing the originator) respond via the portal
  • AI evaluates your response using Gemini + learned examples
  • AI approves or asks for more — NO HUMAN REVIEWER NEEDED

item.name: B1 › 📨 Submit New Transaction — AI Will Handle It

exec: console.log('   The AI is now sending a More Info Request...');
console.log('   Watch server console for 🤖 AI_AUTOMATION lines');
console.log('⚡ Auto-approved (risk score too low for MIR)');
console.log('   Try increasing the amount or add aml_flag:true');

request.description:
Same fingerprint as seeded pattern:
  sec_code = PPD
  transaction_type = debit
  amount = $2,600 (small bucket: $500–$5,000)
  account_type = checking
  risk_flags = none

🤖 WHAT HAPPENS NEXT (automatically, on the server):
  1. checkPatternMatch() finds the promoted pattern
  2. Transaction saved with status = 'ai_workflow'
  3. runAutonomousWorkflow() fires asynchronously
  4. AI picks round 1 from workflow_playbook
  5. AI generates a tailored request message via Gemini
  6. recordAutoMirRequest() creates the portal link
  7. Portal email would be sent (if RESEND_API_KEY set)

Server console lines to watch for:
  🤖 AI_AUTOMATION: Starting autonomous workflow for DEMO...
  🤖 AI_AUTOMATION: Round 1 sent — AUTHORIZATION_PROOF

exec: console.log('📋 Audit Log Events so far:');
console.log('⏳ Wait 2–3 seconds, then run B3 to see the AI MIR request.');

request.description:
📊 CHECK WHAT TO EXPECT:
  status          = 'ai_workflow'
  ai_workflow_pattern = (pattern hash from Phase A)
  ai_human_override   = false

📋 Audit log events you'll see:
  • transaction_created  (SYSTEM)
  • ai_workflow_queued   (AI)
  • ai_workflow_started  (AI_AUTOMATION) ← async, may appear shortly
  • ai_info_requested    (AI_AUTOMATION) ← AI sent the MIR portal!

⏳ TIP: The autonomous workflow fires asynchronously.
Wait 2–3 seconds after B1, then run B3 to see the AI's MIR request.

item.name: B3 › 🤖 See What the AI Asked the Originator

exec: console.log('  ⏳ No MIR rounds yet. Wait 2s and retry B3.');
console.log('✅ AI sent the request! actor_type should be AI_AUTOMATION');
console.log('📌 To get the real portal token: check server console for the link');

request.description:
🤖 THIS IS THE AI'S MIR REQUEST — created with zero human input.

✅ EXPECTED in the response:
  actor_type     = 'AI_AUTOMATION'   ← NOT 'HUMAN'
  category       = from workflow_playbook (e.g. AUTHORIZATION_PROOF)
  message        = AI-generated via Gemini (tailored to this transaction)
  status         = 'pending'
  portal_token   = '••••••••' (masked in API, real token in server console)

📌 HOW TO GET THE PORTAL TOKEN:
  Look at your server console output for a line like:
    ╔══ MIR PORTAL LINK ══╗
    Link: http://localhost:5173/portal/abc123...
  Copy that token (the long hex after /portal/) into {{mirToken}} variable.

item.name: B3b › ⏳ Wait & Retry — If AI Round Not Yet Visible

request.description:
The AI fires asynchronously. If B3 showed 0 rounds, wait 3 seconds and run this.
Keep retrying until actor_type='AI_AUTOMATION' appears.

Normal timing:
  • Gemini API available: ~2–4 seconds
  • Simulation mode (no API key): ~1 second

item.name: ━━ PHASE C: 📮 Originator Responds to AI's Request ━━
item.description:
You are now playing the role of the ORIGINATOR (the company that submitted the transaction).
The AI has sent you a portal link. You open it and submit your response.
The AI will then AUTOMATICALLY evaluate your answer.

item.name: C1 › 🔗 Open the Portal Link (as Originator)

exec: console.log('❌ Invalid token — copy correct token from server console');
console.log('❌ Token expired (72h limit). Re-run the demo.');

request.description:
📌 BEFORE RUNNING:
  Set {{mirToken}} to the actual token from your server console.
  Look for this in console:
    ╔══ MIR PORTAL LINK ══╗
    Link: http://localhost:5173/portal/TOKEN_HERE
  Copy the TOKEN_HERE part → Postman Variables → mirToken

This is the same link the AI emailed to the originator.
✅ EXPECTED: 200 OK with the AI's question and masked transaction details.
Also marks link_opened_at timestamp and writes audit log event: portal_link_opened

item.name: C2 › 📝 Submit Response — Strong Authorization Proof

exec: console.log('🤖 AI IS NOW EVALUATING THE RESPONSE...');
console.log('  AI reads response + learned examples from pattern');
console.log('  Gemini decides: approve / decline / more_info');
console.log('⏳ Wait 3–5 seconds, then run C3 to see AI decision.');

request.description:
📮 ORIGINATOR submits a STRONG response with:
  ✅ Authorization confirmation
  ✅ Reference number
  ✅ Signatory name
  ✅ Contract reference
  ✅ Amount confirmation
  ✅ Word count > 20 (heuristic pass)
  ✅ Contains 'authorized', 'agreement', 'signed'

🤖 WHAT AI DOES NEXT (automatically):
  1. evaluateOriginatorResponse() is called
  2. Gemini LLM reads the response + learned examples from pattern
  3. Heuristic fallback (if no API key): checks keywords + word count
  4. Decides: approve / decline / more_info
  5. If approve: transaction status → 'approved', AI_AUTOMATION reviewer
  6. Audit log: event_type = 'ai_auto_approved'

With a strong response like this, AI should APPROVE automatically.

item.name: C2b › 📝 Alternative: Submit Weak Response (triggers more_info)

request.description:
📮 WEAK RESPONSE — too short, no keywords.

🤖 What AI will likely do:
  Heuristic check: word count < 10 → action = 'more_info'
  AI sends ANOTHER portal round asking for more details.

Use C2 (strong) OR C2b (weak) — not both.
If you use C2b, run B3 again to see the AI's follow-up question.

item.name: C2c › 📝 Alternative: Submit Fraud/Dispute Response (triggers decline)

request.description:
📮 FRAUD/DISPUTE RESPONSE — contains deny keywords.

🤖 What AI will likely do:
  Heuristic detects: 'not authorized', 'fraud', 'never submitted'
  → action = 'decline', confidence = 0.85
  AI declines the transaction automatically.
  Audit log: event_type = 'ai_auto_declined'

Use C2 OR C2b OR C2c — pick one scenario.

item.name: ━━ PHASE D: 🏁 See the AI's Final Decision ━━
item.description:
After the originator responded, the AI evaluated it and made a decision.
Check the transaction status now — the AI acted as the reviewer.

item.name: D1 › 🏁 See Final Transaction Status (AI Decision)

exec: console.log('🎉 DEMO SUCCESS! AI approved the transaction autonomously.');
console.log('   No human reviewer was involved.');
console.log('🎉 DEMO SUCCESS! AI declined the transaction autonomously.');
console.log('🔄 AI asked for another round. Go back and respond via new portal link.');
console.log('⏳ AI is still evaluating. Wait 3s and re-run D1.');

request.description:
🏁 THE MOMENT OF TRUTH.

✅ If AI approved (C2 strong response):
  status          = 'approved'
  reviewer_name   = 'AI Automation'   ← AI was the reviewer!
  reviewer_notes  = 'AI_AUTOMATION: (reason)'
  audit_log shows: ai_auto_approved

❌ If AI declined (C2c fraud response):
  status          = 'declined'
  reviewer_name   = 'AI Automation'
  audit_log shows: ai_auto_declined

🔄 If AI needs more info (C2b weak response):
  status          = 'more_info_required'
  New MIR round created by AI_AUTOMATION
  Run B3 again to see the follow-up question

📋 The audit trail is the full story:
  transaction_created  → SYSTEM
  ai_workflow_queued   → AI
  ai_workflow_started  → AI_AUTOMATION  
  ai_info_requested    → AI_AUTOMATION  (AI sent MIR)
  portal_link_opened   → ORIGINATOR
  originator_response_submitted → ORIGINATOR
  ai_auto_approved     → AI_AUTOMATION  (AI decided!)

↑ Zero human reviewer actions in that chain.

item.name: D2 › 📊 See AI Decision in Review Decisions Table

request.description:
In the response, look at the review_decisions array:

✅ EXPECTED:
  decision        = 'approve' or 'decline'
  reviewer_confidence = 'HIGH'
  confidence_weight   = 1.0
  ai_automation   = true   ← KEY FIELD: confirms AI made this decision
  decision_reason = 'AI_AUTOMATION: (Gemini reasoning)'

This record feeds back into the learning pipeline,
further reinforcing the pattern's confidence score.

item.name: D3 › 📈 Check Pattern Updated After AI Decision

exec: console.log('✅ Pattern learned from this demo run!');
console.log('   Next demo will use this Q&A pair as an example.');

request.description:
After the demo, the pattern should have:
  total_decisions = 6 (5 seeds + 1 AI decision)
  mir_count incremented (AI sent 1 MIR round)
  avg_rounds_to_resolve updated
  learned_qa_pairs has new entry:
    - category: (what AI asked)
    - response_example: (what originator said)
    - final_outcome: 'approve' or 'decline'
    - led_to_decision: true

This Q&A pair will be used as an example in the NEXT demo run,
making the AI smarter with every cycle.

item.name: D4 › 📋 Full Audit Log — Entire AI Workflow Story

request.description:
The complete story in chronological order.

Filter for these event_types to see the AI's actions:
  • ai_workflow_queued       — AI queued
  • ai_workflow_started      — AI began
  • ai_info_requested        — AI sent MIR portal
  • portal_link_opened       — Originator opened link
  • originator_response_submitted — Response received
  • ai_auto_approved         — AI approved ✅
  • ai_auto_declined         — AI declined ❌
  • ai_followup_requested    — AI asked a second round
  • ai_escalated_to_human    — AI gave up, escalated

Every 🤖 line is fully automated — zero human clicks.

item.name: ━━ PHASE E: 🔁 Run the Demo Again ━━
item.description:
Want to show the demo again? The pattern is already promoted.
Just repeat Phase B → C → D. The AI gets smarter each time.

item.name: E1 › Run Demo Again — New Transaction (same pattern)

exec: console.log('🎬 New demo transaction:', j.data.transaction_id, '|', j.data.status);
console.log('Run B2 → B3 → C1 → C2 → D1 to see the AI in action again.');

request.description:
Repeat demo with $3,100 (still small bucket).
AI should now use the Q&A pair from the first demo run as a reference example.
The message it generates will be informed by what worked before.
